blocks.content: It was Cassandra who said it.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I want to see where you work.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: It's where you make things. That's different.
He packed up his sketchbook. She carried Ferdinand.

The walk was only ten minutes, made longer by the rain and shorter by the fact that she asked questions the entire way — not the hollow kind, but the specific kind. What he was working on. Whether he liked painting people or places. Whether he'd ever painted someone without them knowing.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: That's either creepy or very romantic and I genuinely can't decide which.

His apartment was small and organized in the way of someone who had a system no one else would understand. Canvases along the far wall — finished, unfinished, one large landscape two months in progress. A cluttered paint table. Good light from the north window.
Cassandra walked the length of the canvases slowly. He watched her look — really look, the way she had at the Ferdinand painting, as though she was reading something.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Smiles are the hardest thing to get right. People smile differently than they think they do.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I try to paint what's there instead of what's expected.
